```python
# Create the base plate with rounded corners

    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .rect(plate_length, plate_width)
    .extrude(plate_thickness)
    .edges("|Z")
    .fillet(corner_radius)
)

# Add four mounting holes near the corners
hole_x = plate_length / 2 - hole_inset
hole_y = plate_width / 2 - hole_inset

result = (
    result
    .faces(">Z")
    .workplane()
    .pushPoints([
        (hole_x, hole_y),
        (-hole_x, hole_y),
        (hole_x, -hole_y),
        (-hole_x, -hole_y)
    ])
    .hole(hole_diameter, hole_depth)
```

```python
side_notch_height = 10.0

# Create the main plate profile using a 2D sketch
# Working in XZ plane, extruded along Y

# Define the profile with notches
# Starting from bottom-left, going clockwise
# The plate has:
# - Two notches cut from top edge with tabs
# - A notch cut from bottom-right corner
# - A notch cut from right side

# Main body
plate = cq.Workplane("XZ").rect(width, height).extrude(thickness)

# Top edge notches - cut slots from the top
# First top notch (left side of top)
notch1_x = -width/2 + 20.0  # position from center
plate = (plate
    .workplane(offset=0)
    .transformed(offset=cq.Vector(0, 0, 0))
)

# Let me rebuild using a profile approach
# Create profile in XZ plane

points = [
    (0, 0),                              # bottom-left
    (0, height - top_tab_height),         # up left side to below top tabs
    (15, height - top_tab_height),        # along to first tab start
    (15, height),                         # up to top of first tab
    (30, height),                         # across first tab top
    (30, height - top_tab_height),        # down from first tab
    (55, height - top_tab_height),        # across gap to second tab
    (55, height),                         # up to top of second tab
    (75, height),                         # across second tab top
    (75, height - top_tab_height),        # down from second tab
    (width, height - top_tab_height),     # across to right edge
    (width, bottom_tab_height + bottom_notch_depth + side_notch_height + 10),  # down right side
    (width, bottom_tab_height + bottom_notch_depth + side_notch_height),       # continue down
    (width - side_notch_width, bottom_tab_height + bottom_notch_depth + side_notch_height),  # notch in
    (width - side_notch_width, bottom_tab_height + bottom_notch_depth),        # notch down
    (width, bottom_tab_height + bottom_notch_depth),                            # notch out
    (width, bottom_tab_height),           # down to bottom tab area
    (width, 0),                           # to bottom-right corner
    (width - 12, 0),                      # along bottom
    (width - 12, bottom_tab_height),      # up for bottom-right tab
    (width - 25, bottom_tab_height),      # across
    (width - 25, 0),                      # back down
    (12, 0),                              # along bottom to left notch area  
    (12, bottom_tab_height),              # up
    (0, bottom_tab_height),               # across to left edge
]

# Simplified approach - main plate with cuts
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(width, 0)
    .lineTo(width, height - top_tab_height)
    .lineTo(75, height - top_tab_height)
    .lineTo(75, height)
    .lineTo(55, height)
    .lineTo(55, height - top_tab_height)
    .lineTo(30, height - top_tab_height)
    .lineTo(30, height)
    .lineTo(15, height)
    .lineTo(15, height - top_tab_height)
    .lineTo(0, height - top_tab_height)
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Cut bottom-left notch
result = (result
    .workplane(offset=0)
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .rect(15, bottom_tab_height, centered=False)
    .cutBlind(thickness)
)

# Cut bottom-right notch

    .workplane(offset=0)
    .moveTo(width - 15, 0)

)

# Cut right-side notch (middle of right edge)

    .workplane(offset=0)
    .moveTo(width - side_notch_width, 25)
    .rect(side_notch_width, side_notch_height, centered=False)
    .cutBlind(thickness)
)
```

```python
    ])
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
thickness = 4.0
center_hole_radius = 25.0
bolt_hole_radius = 6.0
bolt_hole_distance = 45.0  # distance from center to bolt hole centers
lug_radius = 12.0  # radius of the lug circles around bolt holes
outer_radius = 35.0  # outer radius of the central ring area

# Create the main body as a 2D sketch extruded
# Start with the central oval/ring shape with two lugs

# Create the outline by combining:
# 1. A large circle for the center body
# 2. Two smaller circles (lugs) at each end for bolt holes
# Then subtract the center hole and bolt holes

# Main body circle
body = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(outer_radius).extrude(thickness)

# Add lug 1 (positive X direction along the diagonal)
lug1 = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .center(bolt_hole_distance, 0)
        .circle(lug_radius)
        .extrude(thickness))

# Add lug 2 (negative X direction)
lug2 = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .center(-bolt_hole_distance, 0)
        .circle(lug_radius)
        .extrude(thickness))

# Union all solid parts
result = body.union(lug1).union(lug2)

# Cut the center hole
result = (result
          .faces(">Z")
          .workplane()
          .circle(center_hole_radius)
          .cutThruAll())

# Cut bolt hole 1
result = (result
          .faces(">Z")
          .workplane()
          .center(bolt_hole_distance, 0)
          .circle(bolt_hole_radius)
          .cutThruAll())

# Cut bolt hole 2
result = (result
          .faces(">Z")
          .workplane()
          .center(-bolt_hole_distance, 0)
          .circle(bolt_hole_radius)
          .cutThruAll())
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
length = 200.0        # Total length of the flat bar
width = 30.0          # Width of the flat bar
thickness = 5.0       # Thickness of the flat bar
hole_diameter = 8.0   # Diameter of the holes
num_holes = 7         # Number of holes
edge_margin = 15.0    # Distance from edge to center of first/last hole

# Calculate hole spacing
hole_spacing = (length - 2 * edge_margin) / (num_holes - 1)

# Create the flat bar
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(length, width, thickness)
)

# Add holes evenly spaced along the length
for i in range(num_holes):
    x_pos = -length / 2 + edge_margin + i * hole_spacing
    result = (
        result
        .faces(">Z")
        .workplane()
        .moveTo(x_pos, 0)
        .hole(hole_diameter)
    )
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
base_length = 100  # X direction
base_width = 70    # Y direction
base_height = 15   # Z direction

# Start with the main base block
result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(base_length, base_width, base_height)

# Cut out the front-left notch (cross-shaped cutout on the left side)
# There's a rectangular cutout on the front-left
notch_width = 25
notch_depth = 25
result = result.workplane().move(-base_length/2 + notch_width/2, -base_width/2 + notch_depth/2).rect(notch_width, notch_depth).cutBlind(-base_height)

# Cut out the back-left notch
result = (result.workplane()
          .move(-base_length/2 + notch_width/2, base_width/2 - notch_depth/2)
          .rect(notch_width, notch_depth)
          .cutBlind(-base_height))

# Central channel/pocket - the recessed area in the middle-top
channel_length = 60
channel_width = 40
channel_depth = 8
result = (result.faces(">Z").workplane()
          .move(5, 0)
          .rect(channel_length, channel_width)
          .cutBlind(-channel_depth))

# Raised walls on the back side (the two tall blocks along the back edge)
wall_thickness = 10
wall_height = 12
wall_length = 60

# Back wall - left section
result = (result.faces(">Z").workplane()
          .move(5, base_width/2 - wall_thickness/2 - 5)
          .rect(wall_length, wall_thickness)
          .extrude(wall_height))

# Corner blocks/pillars - four raised blocks at corners of the channel
pillar_size = 10
pillar_height = 18

# Back-left pillar
result = (result.faces(">Z").workplane(offset=-wall_height)
          .move(-base_length/2 + 28, base_width/2 - 12)
          .rect(pillar_size, pillar_size)
          .extrude(pillar_height))

# Back-center pillar (taller one)
result = (result.faces(">Z").workplane(offset=-pillar_height - wall_height + wall_height)
          .move(5, base_width/2 - 15)
          .rect(pillar_size, pillar_size)
          .extrude(pillar_height + 5))

# Front-right raised block
result = (result.faces(">Z").workplane(offset=0)
          .center(base_length/2 - 18, -base_width/2 + 18)
          .rect(pillar_size, pillar_size)
          .extrude(pillar_height))

# Reconstruct with cleaner approach
result = cq.Workplane("XY")

# Main base plate
base = cq.Workplane("XY").box(base_length, base_width, base_height)

# Remove front-left corner
base = base.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY").move(-base_length/2 + 12.5, -base_width/2 + 12.5).rect(25, 25).extrude(base_height).translate((0, 0, -base_height/2))
)

# Remove back-left corner  
base = base.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY").move(-base_length/2 + 12.5, base_width/2 - 12.5).rect(25, 25).extrude(base_height).translate((0, 0, -base_height/2))
)

# Central pocket
base = base.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY").transformed(offset=(5, 0, base_height/2 - channel_depth)).rect(channel_length, channel_width).extrude(channel_depth)
)

# Add raised rail on the back
base = base.union(
    cq.Workplane("XY").transformed(offset=(5, 0, base_height/2)).rect(channel_length + 10, wall_thickness).extrude(wall_height).translate((0, base_width/2 - wall_thickness/2 - 3, 0))
)

# Add corner pillars
for x, y in [(-15, 20), (10, 22), (30, 20), (35, -18)]:
    base = base.union(
        cq.Workplane("XY").transformed(offset=(x, y, base_height/2)).rect(10, 10).extrude(15)
    )

# Small block in center of pocket
base = base.union(
    cq.Workplane("XY").transformed(offset=(0, 0, base_height/2 - channel_depth)).rect(10, 10).extrude(channel_depth - 2)
)

# Central hole
base = base.faces(">Z").workplane().pushPoints([(0, 0)]).hole(6, 20)

result = base
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
total_width = 80  # X direction
total_depth = 60  # Y direction
total_height = 60  # Z direction

notch_width = 30  # width of the rectangular notch cut from top-left
notch_depth = 30  # depth of the notch

cylinder_radius = 20  # radius of the cylindrical cutout
cylinder_depth = 60  # depth of the cylinder cut (full depth)

# Start with the main block
result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(total_width, total_depth, total_height)

# Cut the rectangular notch from the top-left area
# The notch is on the top, at the left-back corner
notch = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(-(total_width - notch_width) / 2, (total_depth - notch_depth) / 2, (total_height) / 2 - notch_depth / 2))
    .box(notch_width, notch_depth, notch_depth)
)

# Position the notch at the top-left-back
result = result - notch

# Redefine the notch more carefully
# The block is centered at origin. 
# Top face is at z = 30, bottom at z = -30
# Left face at x = -40, right at x = 40
# Front face at y = -30, back at y = 30

# The V-shaped notch from the top: it's a rectangular cut from the top
# Looking at the image: there's a rectangular step cut from the top-back-left
notch_height = 25  # height of the notch from the top
notch_x = 35  # width of notch in X

result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(total_width, total_depth, total_height)

# Cut rectangular notch from top - positioned at left-back area
notch_block = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(
        -total_width/2 + notch_x/2,  # left side
        total_depth/2 - notch_depth/2,  # back side
        total_height/2 - notch_height/2  # top
    ))
    .box(notch_x, notch_depth, notch_height)
)

result = result - notch_block

# Cut the cylindrical groove from the front face
# The cylinder runs along Y axis (front to back), positioned at the left side
# Center of cylinder appears to be at the junction of the step
cylinder_center_x = -total_width/2 + notch_x
cylinder_center_z = total_height/2 - notch_height

cylinder_cut = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .transformed(offset=(cylinder_center_x, cylinder_center_z, 0))
    .circle(cylinder_radius)
    .extrude(total_depth/2 + 1, both=True)
)

result = result - cylinder_cut
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
length = 200.0  # total length of the bracket
width = 30.0    # width of the bracket
thickness = 3.0  # thickness of the plate

# Curvature - the plate has a slight bow/curve
bow_depth = 12.0  # how much the plate bows outward

# Hole parameters
hole_diameter = 10.0  # diameter of the circular holes

# Rectangular slot parameters
slot_length = 12.0
slot_width = 8.0

# Create the curved plate profile
# We'll create it as a lofted/swept shape with curvature
# First, create the base outline as a 2D shape, then add curvature

# Create the flat plate outline - elongated shape with rounded ends
plate = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(width, length, thickness)
)

# Create the curved shape by making a lens/lenticular cross-section
# The plate bows outward - thicker in the middle, thinner at the ends
curved_plate = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(-length/2, 0)
    .threePointArc((0, bow_depth), (length/2, 0))
    .threePointArc((0, -thickness), (-length/2, 0))
    .close()
    .extrude(width/2, both=True)
)

# Round the top and bottom ends
curved_plate = (
    curved_plate
    .edges("|Y").fillet(1.5)
)

# Add the two circular holes - positioned symmetrically
hole_y_offset = 45.0  # distance from center for each hole

curved_plate = (
    curved_plate
    .faces(">Y")
    .workplane()
    .pushPoints([(0, hole_y_offset), (0, -hole_y_offset)])
    .hole(hole_diameter)
)

# Add rectangular slots near the ends
slot_y_offset = 82.0  # distance from center for slots

# Top slot
curved_plate = (
    curved_plate
    .faces(">Y")
    .workplane()
    .pushPoints([(0, slot_y_offset), (0, -slot_y_offset)])
    .rect(slot_width, slot_length)
    .cutThruAll()
)

# Add a notch/cutout at the very top
notch_width = 8.0
notch_depth = 5.0

curved_plate = (
    curved_plate
    .faces(">Y")
    .workplane()
    .center(0, length/2 - notch_depth/2)
    .rect(notch_width, notch_depth)
    .cutThruAll()
)

result = curved_plate
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
outer_radius = 50
inner_radius = 30
wall_thickness = outer_radius - inner_radius  # 20
height = 20
arm_length = 50
arm_width = wall_thickness  # 20

# Create the C-shaped arc (270 degrees, open at the top-right)
# The arc goes from the top of the left side, around the bottom, to the right side
# Create the profile as a 2D sketch: an annular sector

# Build the C-shape using a 2D profile extruded
# The opening is at the top, between the two arms

# Create outer arc and inner arc to form C-shape
# Arc spans 270 degrees (3/4 of a circle), opening facing up-right

# Let's place center of the arc at origin
# The C-shape opens at the top
# Arc from 0 degrees to 270 degrees (counterclockwise)

import math

# Create the C-shaped ring (270 degrees)
# Opening is at the top between the straight arm and the right end
c_shape = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(rotate=(0, 0, 0))
    .move(outer_radius, 0)
    .radiusArc((0, outer_radius), -outer_radius)
    .radiusArc((-outer_radius, 0), -outer_radius)
    .radiusArc((0, -outer_radius), -outer_radius)
    .lineTo(0, -inner_radius)
    .radiusArc((-inner_radius, 0), inner_radius)
    .radiusArc((0, inner_radius), inner_radius)
    .radiusArc((inner_radius, 0), inner_radius)
    .close()
    .extrude(height)
)

# Add the straight arm extending from the top-left of the C-shape
# The arm extends from the left end of the C upward (in the +Y direction)
arm = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(0, 0, 0))
    .rect(arm_width, arm_length, centered=False)
    .translate((-arm_width, 0, 0))
    .extrude(height)
)

# Combine
result = c_shape + arm
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# L-shaped block - created by subtracting a box from a larger box
# Overall dimensions
length = 80
width = 40
height = 40

# Step dimensions
step_length = 40
step_width = 20
step_height = 15

# Create the base block
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(length, width, height, centered=False)
)

# Cut out the step from the top-left corner
result = (
    result
    .workplane(offset=0)
    .transformed(offset=cq.Vector(0, width - step_width, height - step_height))
    .box(step_length, step_width, step_height, centered=False, combine=False)
)

# Actually, let me redo this more cleanly with an L-shaped profile
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(length, 0)
    .lineTo(length, height)
    .lineTo(step_length, height)
    .lineTo(step_length, height - step_height)
    .lineTo(0, height - step_height)
    .close()
    .extrude(width)
)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq
```

```python
# This is a regular dodecahedron - a polyhedron with 12 pentagonal faces
# We'll create it by starting with a cube and cutting corners appropriately
# Using the mathematical approach: intersection of half-spaces

import math

# Golden ratio
phi = (1 + math.sqrt(5)) / 2
size = 30  # Scale factor

# A regular dodecahedron can be created by chamfering/cutting a cube,
# or by intersecting half-spaces defined by the face normals.
# The 12 face normals of a regular dodecahedron point toward:
# (±1, ±1, ±1), (0, ±1/phi, ±phi), (±1/phi, ±phi, 0), (±phi, 0, ±1/phi)

# We'll build it as intersection of half-spaces using CadQuery
# Start with a large box and cut with planes

def make_dodecahedron(edge_size=30):
    # Face normals of a regular dodecahedron (not normalized)
    normals = []
    
    # 4 faces with normals (±1, ±1, ±1) - but only specific combinations
    # Actually, the cube-type normals are NOT part of dodecahedron
    # Dodecahedron face normals are:
    # (0, ±1, ±phi), (±1, ±phi, 0), (±phi, 0, ±1)
    
    for s1 in [1, -1]:
        for s2 in [1, -1]:
            normals.append((0, s1 * 1, s2 * phi))
            normals.append((s1 * 1, s2 * phi, 0))
            normals.append((s1 * phi, 0, s2 * 1))
    
    # Distance from center to each face for a dodecahedron with unit edge length
    # d = phi^2 / sqrt(3) for unit edge
    # We'll use a scale factor
    
    # Normalize normals and compute distance
    # For a regular dodecahedron, the distance from center to face = 
    # (phi^2) / sqrt(3) * edge_length / 2... let's just use a consistent distance
    
    # The distance from origin to each face when normals have magnitude sqrt(1+phi^2)
    # is phi^2 (for edge length = 2)
    
    dist = edge_size  # distance from center to face plane
    
    # Start with a large box
    result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(edge_size * 4, edge_size * 4, edge_size * 4)
    
    for nx, ny, nz in normals:
        # Normalize the normal
        length = math.sqrt(nx**2 + ny**2 + nz**2)
        nnx, nny, nnz = nx/length, ny/length, nz/length
        
        # Create a cutting box - a large box positioned so that cutting removes
        # everything beyond the plane at distance 'dist' from origin along the normal
        
        # Point on the plane
        px, py, pz = nnx * dist, nny * dist, nnz * dist
        
        # We need to cut a half-space. We'll use a large box centered far along the normal
        cut_depth = edge_size * 4
        box_size = edge_size * 8
        
        # Center of cutting box is at distance (dist + cut_depth/2) along normal
        cx = nnx * (dist + cut_depth/2)
        cy = nny * (dist + cut_depth/2)
        cz = nnz * (dist + cut_depth/2)
        
        # Create cutting solid using CQ
        cutter = (cq.Workplane("XY")
                  .box(box_size, box_size, cut_depth)
                  .translate((cx, cy, cz)))
        
        # Rotate the cutter to align with the normal
        # We need to orient the thin dimension along the normal direction
        
    # Alternative approach: use vertices directly
    # Vertices of a regular dodecahedron:
    # (±1, ±1, ±1), (0, ±1/phi, ±phi), (±phi, 0, ±1/phi), (±1/phi, ±phi, 0)
    
    verts = []
    for s1 in [1, -1]:
        for s2 in [1, -1]:
            for s3 in [1, -1]:
                verts.append((s1 * size, s2 * size, s3 * size))
    for s1 in [1, -1]:
        for s2 in [1, -1]:
            verts.append((0, s1 * size / phi, s2 * size * phi))
            verts.append((s1 * size * phi, 0, s2 * size / phi))
            verts.append((s1 * size / phi, s2 * size * phi, 0))
    
    # Use CadQuery's hull
    wp = cq.Workplane("XY")
    solid = cq.Shape.makeBox(1, 1, 1)  # dummy
    
    # Build using hull of points
    from OCP.BRepBuilderAPI import BRepBuilderAPI_MakeVertex
    from OCP.gp import gp_Pnt
    from OCP.BRepAlgoAPI import BRepAlgoAPI_Fuse
    
    # Use CadQuery's findSolid with hull approach
    points = [cq.Vector(v[0], v[1], v[2]) for v in verts]
    
    hull = cq.Shape.makeBox(0.01, 0.01, 0.01)
    
    return verts

# Create dodecahedron using convex hull via CadQuery
verts = make_dodecahedron(30)

# Use Solid.makeConvexHull or manual approach
result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(0.001, 0.001, 0.001)

# Build convex hull using OCP
from OCP.gp import gp_Pnt
from OCP.BRep import BRep_Builder
from OCP.TopoDS import TopoDS_Compound
from OCP.BRepBuilderAPI import BRepBuilderAPI_MakeVertex

builder = BRep_Builder()
compound = TopoDS_Compound()
builder.MakeCompound(compound)

for v in verts:
    vertex = BRepBuilderAPI_MakeVertex(gp_Pnt(v[0], v[1], v[2])).Vertex()
    builder.Add(compound, vertex)

from OCP.BRepAlgo import BRepAlgo_ConvexDecomposition  # may not exist

# Use QuickHull approach via ConvexHull from scipy and build faces
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial import ConvexHull

points = np.array(verts)
hull = ConvexHull(points)

# Build solid from hull faces
from OCP.BRepBuilderAPI import (BRepBuilderAPI_MakePolygon, 
                                 BRepBuilderAPI_MakeFace,
                                 BRepBuilderAPI_Sewing,
                                 BRepBuilderAPI_MakeSolid)
from OCP.BRep import BRep_Builder
from OCP.TopoDS import TopoDS_Shell, TopoDS_Solid

sewing = BRepBuilderAPI_Sewing(1e-6)

for simplex in hull.simplices:
    p1 = gp_Pnt(float(points[simplex[0]][0]), float(points[simplex[0]][1]), float(points[simplex[0]][2]))
    p2 = gp_Pnt(float(points[simplex[1]][0]), float(points[simplex[1]][1]), float(points[simplex[1]][2]))
    p3 = gp_Pnt(float(points[simplex[2]][0]), float(points[simplex[2]][1]), float(points[simplex[2]][2]))
    
    wire_builder = BRepBuilderAPI_MakePolygon(p1, p2, p3, True)
    wire = wire_builder.Wire()
    face = BRepBuilderAPI_MakeFace(wire, True).Face()
    sewing.Add(face)

sewing.Perform()
sewn_shape = sewing.SewedShape()

from OCP.BRepBuilderAPI import BRepBuilderAPI_MakeSolid
from OCP.TopoDS import TopoDS
from OCP.ShapeFix import ShapeFix_Solid

shell = TopoDS.Shell_s(sewn_shape)
solid_maker = BRepBuilderAPI_MakeSolid(shell)
solid = solid_maker.Solid()

fixer = ShapeFix_Solid(solid)
fixer.Perform()
fixed_solid = fixer.Solid()

result = cq.Workplane("XY").newObject([cq.Shape(fixed_solid)])
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
length = 100  # overall length (X)
width = 70    # overall width (Y)
height = 15   # overall height (Z)

# Notch parameters
notch_size = 15  # square notch size
notch_depth = notch_size  # depth of notch into the body

result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(length, width, height)

# Cut notches from all four corners
# Each corner has a rectangular notch cut out

# Top-left corner (negative X, positive Y)
result = result.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(-length/2 + notch_size/2, width/2 - notch_size/2, 0))
    .box(notch_size, notch_size, height)
)

# Top-right corner (positive X, positive Y)
result = result.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(length/2 - notch_size/2, width/2 - notch_size/2, 0))
    .box(notch_size, notch_size, height)
)

# Bottom-left corner (negative X, negative Y)
result = result.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(-length/2 + notch_size/2, -width/2 + notch_size/2, 0))
    .box(notch_size, notch_size, height)
)

# Bottom-right corner (positive X, negative Y)
result = result.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(length/2 - notch_size/2, -width/2 + notch_size/2, 0))
    .box(notch_size, notch_size, height)
)

# Cut notches from the middle of each edge
mid_notch_width = 18
mid_notch_depth = 12

# Middle of top edge (positive Y)
result = result.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(0, width/2 - mid_notch_depth/2, 0))
    .box(mid_notch_width, mid_notch_depth, height)
)

# Middle of bottom edge (negative Y)
result = result.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(0, -width/2 + mid_notch_depth/2, 0))
    .box(mid_notch_width, mid_notch_depth, height)
)

# Middle of left edge (negative X)
result = result.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(-length/2 + mid_notch_depth/2, 0, 0))
    .box(mid_notch_depth, mid_notch_width, height)
)

# Middle of right edge (positive X)
result = result.cut(
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(length/2 - mid_notch_depth/2, 0, 0))
    .box(mid_notch_depth, mid_notch_width, height)
)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
length = 250.0
flange_width = 20.0
web_height = 20.0
thickness = 3.0

# Hole parameters
hole_diameter = 4.0
hole_count = 3
hole_margin = 30.0  # distance from ends to first/last hole

# Create L-angle profile by extruding an L-shaped cross section
# The L-shape consists of a vertical web and a horizontal flange
profile = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(flange_width, 0)
    .lineTo(flange_width, thickness)
    .lineTo(thickness, thickness)
    .lineTo(thickness, web_height)
    .lineTo(0, web_height)
    .close()
)

# Extrude along the length
angle = profile.extrude(length)

# Add holes on the vertical web face
# Holes are spaced evenly along the length on the vertical flange
hole_spacing = (length - 2 * hole_margin) / (hole_count - 1)

# Drill holes through the vertical web
angle = (
    angle
    .faces(">Y")  # select the outer face of the vertical web
    .workplane()
    .center(0, 0)
    .pushPoints([
        (-length / 2 + hole_margin + i * hole_spacing, 0)
        for i in range(hole_count)
    ])
    .hole(hole_diameter, thickness)
)

result = angle
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
thickness = 12  # depth/thickness of the part
base_length = 100  # length of the bottom edge
base_height = 40  # height of the rectangular lower portion
total_height = 90  # total height at the tall end
hole_diameter = 14  # diameter of the hole
hole_x = 25  # hole center x position from left
hole_y = 20  # hole center y position from bottom

# Top ridge/tab parameters
ridge_height = 5
ridge_width = 8
ridge_setback = 3  # setback from front face

# Create the main body profile (viewed from front)
# The shape is like a right triangle on top of a rectangle
# Bottom-left corner is origin
main_profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(base_length, 0)
    .lineTo(base_length, total_height)
    .lineTo(base_length - 10, total_height)  # small top edge
    .lineTo(0, base_height)  # angled line going down to the left
    .lineTo(0, 0)
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Add the hole (through hole)
main_body = (
    main_profile
    .faces(">Z").workplane(centerOption="CenterOfBoundBox")
    .center(-base_length/2 + hole_x, -total_height/2 + hole_y)
    .hole(hole_diameter)
)

# Create the ridge/tab on top
# The ridge runs along the top sloped edge, near the tall end
ridge_length = 30
ridge_start_x = base_length - ridge_length - 5

# Create ridge on the top surface near the right/tall end
ridge = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(ridge_start_x, total_height - 8)
    .lineTo(ridge_start_x + ridge_length, total_height - 2)
    .lineTo(base_length - 5, total_height)
    .lineTo(base_length - 5, total_height + ridge_height)
    .lineTo(base_length - 15, total_height + ridge_height)
    .lineTo(base_length - 18, total_height + ridge_height - 2)
    .lineTo(ridge_start_x + 5, total_height - 2)
    .close()
    .extrude(ridge_width)
    .translate((0, 0, (thickness - ridge_width) / 2))
)

# Create a step/ledge feature on top near the tall end
# This appears to be a raised section with a groove/slot
step = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(base_length - 25, total_height - 5)
    .lineTo(base_length, total_height - 5)
    .lineTo(base_length, total_height + 4)
    .lineTo(base_length - 5, total_height + 4)
    .lineTo(base_length - 8, total_height + 1)
    .lineTo(base_length - 20, total_height + 1)
    .lineTo(base_length - 25, total_height - 2)
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Combine - just use the main body with the step feature
# Simplified approach: create the complete profile

# Let me redo this more cleanly
result_body = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(base_length, 0)
    .lineTo(base_length, total_height)
    .lineTo(base_length - 5, total_height)
    .lineTo(0, base_height)
    .lineTo(0, 0)
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Cut the hole
result_body = (
    result_body
    .faces("<Y").workplane(centerOption="CenterOfBoundBox")
    .center(-base_length/2 + hole_x, 0)
    .hole(hole_diameter)
)

# Add raised tab/ridge on top at the tall end
tab = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(base_length - 30, total_height)
    .lineTo(base_length, total_height)
    .lineTo(base_length, total_height + ridge_height)
    .lineTo(base_length - 8, total_height + ridge_height)
    .lineTo(base_length - 30, total_height)
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness * 0.6)
    .translate((0, 0, thickness * 0.2))
)

# Cut a groove in the tab
groove = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(base_length - 20, total_height + 1)
    .lineTo(base_length - 10, total_height + 1)
    .lineTo(base_length - 10, total_height + ridge_height + 1)
    .lineTo(base_length - 20, total_height + ridge_height + 1)
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)

tab = tab.cut(groove)

result = result_body.union(tab)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
width = 80    # depth (Y direction)
height = 60   # total height (Z direction)
length = 80   # base length (X direction)
top_length = 30  # flat top portion length
radius = 70   # radius of the curved front face

# Create the basic rectangular block
block = cq.Workplane("XY").box(length, width, height, centered=False)

# Create a cylinder to cut the curved surface
# The curve goes from the bottom-front edge up to where the flat top starts
# We need a quarter-circle-like curve on the XZ plane

# The curved face goes from (0, 0, 0) at the bottom to approximately (length - top_length, 0, height) at the top
# This is a concave curve on the front face

# Create the cutting tool - a cylinder oriented along Y axis
# The curve radius and position need to create the concave scoop shape
# The cylinder center should be positioned so it carves out the front-left portion

# Using a spline/arc approach with extrusion
# The profile is in the XZ plane: starts at bottom (0,0), curves to top (length-top_length, height)

# Create the side profile as a 2D sketch
profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(length, 0)  # bottom edge
    .lineTo(length, height)  # right side going up
    .lineTo(length - top_length, height)  # top flat surface
    .radiusArc((0, 0), -radius)  # curved front face back to start
    .close()
    .extrude(width)
)

result = profile
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
body_length = 60.0  # X direction
body_width = 50.0   # Y direction
body_height = 35.0  # Z direction

# Create the main body block
result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(body_length, body_width, body_height)

# Add the raised sections on top (two raised pads on either side of center groove)
pad_width = 18.0  # width of each raised pad in Y
pad_height = 8.0   # additional height above main body
pad_length = 60.0  # full length

# Left raised pad
left_pad = (cq.Workplane("XY")
            .center(0, -(body_width/2 - pad_width/2))
            .box(pad_length, pad_width, body_height + pad_height)
            .translate((0, 0, pad_height/2)))

# Right raised pad
right_pad = (cq.Workplane("XY")
             .center(0, (body_width/2 - pad_width/2))
             .box(pad_length, pad_width, body_height + pad_height)
             .translate((0, 0, pad_height/2)))

result = result.union(left_pad).union(right_pad)

# Create the concave groove/channel in the center top (curved saddle shape)
groove_radius = 18.0
groove_depth = pad_height + 5.0

# Create a cylinder to cut the groove along X axis at the top
groove_cutter = (cq.Workplane("YZ")
                 .center(0, body_height/2 + pad_height + groove_radius - groove_depth)
                 .circle(groove_radius)
                 .extrude(body_length + 10, both=True))

# Move the groove cutter to center
result = result.cut(groove_cutter)

# Create the clevis/fork feature on the right side (+X direction)
# First, cut a slot through the body to create two prongs
slot_width = 16.0   # gap between the fork prongs
slot_depth = 25.0   # how deep the slot goes from the right end
slot_height = body_height + pad_height + 10  # full height cut

slot_cutter = (cq.Workplane("XY")
               .center(body_length/2 - slot_depth/2 + 2, 0)
               .box(slot_depth + 4, slot_width, slot_height))

result = result.cut(slot_cutter)

# Add the pin hole through the clevis prongs
hole_diameter = 10.0
hole_center_x = body_length/2 - 8.0
hole_center_z = body_height/2 - 2.0

# Drill hole through Y axis at the clevis location
result = (result.faces(">X")
          .workplane()
          .center(0, hole_center_z - body_height/2 + 5)
          .hole(hole_diameter, depth=body_width + 10))

# Actually, let's redo the hole more precisely through the fork prongs
result2 = cq.Workplane("XY").box(body_length, body_width, body_height)

# Raised pads
pad_offset_y = (body_width - pad_width) / 2
result2 = (result2
           .union(cq.Workplane("XY").transformed(offset=(0, -pad_offset_y, pad_height/2)).box(pad_length, pad_width, body_height + pad_height))
           .union(cq.Workplane("XY").transformed(offset=(0, pad_offset_y, pad_height/2)).box(pad_length, pad_width, body_height + pad_height)))

# Cut the saddle groove
result2 = result2.cut(groove_cutter)

# Cut the fork slot
fork_slot = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
             .center(body_length/2 - slot_depth/2, 0)
             .rect(slot_depth + 1, body_height + pad_height + 20)
             .extrude(slot_width/2, both=True))

result2 = result2.cut(fork_slot)

# Drill pin hole through the fork prongs (along Y axis)
result2 = (result2.faces(">Y")
           .workplane()
           .center(-(body_length/2 - 10), 3)
           .hole(hole_diameter, body_width + 10))

# Chamfer the bottom left edge
result2 = result2.edges("|Y").edges("<X").edges("<Z").chamfer(8.0)

result = result2
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
plate_width = 80.0
plate_height = 50.0
plate_thickness = 5.0

cylinder_radius = 10.0
cylinder_length = 70.0

slot_width = 10.0
slot_height = 25.0

# Create the main flat plate
plate = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .rect(plate_width, plate_height)
    .extrude(plate_thickness)
)

# Move plate so its front face is at y=0
plate = plate.translate((0, -plate_thickness/2, 0))

# Create the rectangular slot/notch cut from the top-right of the plate
slot = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(plate_width/2 - slot_width/2, plate_height/2 - slot_height/2)
    .rect(slot_width, slot_height)
    .extrude(plate_thickness + 2)
)
slot = slot.translate((0, -plate_thickness/2 - 1, 0))

plate = plate.cut(slot)

# Create the cylindrical handle extending from the left side of the plate
# The cylinder extends from the left edge of the plate, going to the left
# It's positioned at the bottom of the plate
cylinder = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(-plate_width/2, 0, -plate_height/2 + cylinder_radius))
    .circle(cylinder_radius)
    .extrude(-cylinder_length)
)

# Create a small flat base/tab under the cylinder connecting to plate
base_tab = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-plate_width/2, -plate_thickness/2)
    .rect(cylinder_length, plate_thickness, centered=False)
    .extrude(-plate_height/2 + cylinder_radius)
)
# Reposition the base tab
base_tab_actual = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(-plate_width/2 - cylinder_length/2, -plate_height/2 + plate_thickness/2)
    .rect(cylinder_length, plate_thickness)
    .extrude(plate_thickness)
)
base_tab_actual = base_tab_actual.translate((0, -plate_thickness/2, 0))

# Combine plate with base and cylinder
# The cylinder sits on top of the base tab at the left side
# Let me reconsider the geometry - it's an L-shaped bracket with a cylinder

# Start over with cleaner approach
result_plate = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .rect(plate_width, plate_height)
    .extrude(plate_thickness)
    .translate((0, -plate_thickness / 2, 0))
)

# Bottom horizontal arm extending to the left
arm_length = cylinder_length
arm_height = plate_thickness
arm = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-plate_width/2 - arm_length/2, 0)
    .rect(arm_length, plate_thickness)
    .extrude(plate_thickness)
    .translate((0, -plate_thickness/2, -plate_height/2))
)

# Cylinder on top of the arm, along its length
cylinder_part = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .transformed(offset=(0, 0, -plate_width/2 - arm_length))
    .workplane()
    .center(0, -plate_height/2 + plate_thickness + cylinder_radius)
    .circle(cylinder_radius)
    .extrude(arm_length)
)

# Simpler cylinder approach
cylinder_part = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .center(-plate_width/2, 0)
    .circle(cylinder_radius)
    .extrude(-arm_length)
    .translate((0, 0, -plate_height/2 + plate_thickness + cylinder_radius))
)

# Slot cut
slot_cut = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(plate_width/2 - slot_width, plate_height/2 - slot_height/2)
    .rect(slot_width, slot_height)
    .extrude(plate_thickness * 2)
    .translate((0, -plate_thickness, 0))
)

# Combine everything
result = result_plate.union(arm).union(cylinder_part).cut(slot_cut)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
total_length = 150.0  # overall length center-to-center of holes
bar_width = 8.0  # width of the connecting bar
bar_thickness = 4.0  # thickness of the bar
end_radius = 10.0  # radius of the circular ends
hole_radius = 3.0  # radius of the mounting holes
end_thickness = 5.0  # thickness of the end pads (slightly thicker than bar)

# Create the link bar as a 2D profile extruded
# Start with a stadium/slot shape - two circles connected by a rectangle
bar_profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-total_length / 2, 0)
    .circle(end_radius)
    .moveTo(total_length / 2, 0)
    .circle(end_radius)
    .rect(total_length, bar_width)
    .extrude(bar_thickness)
)

# Better approach - create using hull-like shape
# Create the main bar body as a slot shape
bar = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .slot2D(total_length + 2 * end_radius, bar_width, angle=0)
    .extrude(bar_thickness)
)

# Create the end pads (circular bosses at each end)
end_pad_1 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .center(-total_length / 2, 0)
    .circle(end_radius)
    .extrude(end_thickness)
)

end_pad_2 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .center(total_length / 2, 0)
    .circle(end_radius)
    .extrude(end_thickness)
)

# Union all parts
result = bar.union(end_pad_1).union(end_pad_2)

# Cut the mounting holes through everything
result = (
    result
    .faces(">Z")
    .workplane()
    .pushPoints([(-total_length / 2, 0), (total_length / 2, 0)])
    .hole(hole_radius * 2, end_thickness)
)

# Add chamfer/fillet to edges for a nicer look
result = result.edges("|Z").fillet(0.5)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
plate_length = 120.0
plate_width = 100.0
plate_thickness = 5.0

# Inner cutout parameters
cutout_length = 70.0
cutout_width = 55.0

# Raised frame parameters
frame_outer_length = 100.0
frame_outer_width = 80.0
frame_thickness = 8.0
frame_height = 5.0

# Boss parameters
boss_radius = 8.0
boss_height = 10.0
hole_radius = 3.0
hole_depth = 10.0

# Positions of bosses (at corners of the plate)
boss_x_offset = plate_length / 2 - 12.0
boss_y_offset = plate_width / 2 - 12.0

# Create the base plate
base_plate = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(plate_length, plate_width, plate_thickness, centered=(True, True, False))
)

# Create the raised frame on top of the base plate
raised_frame = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=plate_thickness)
    .box(frame_outer_length, frame_outer_width, frame_height, centered=(True, True, False))
)

# Cut the inner opening from the raised frame
frame_wall = 10.0
inner_cut_length = frame_outer_length - 2 * frame_wall
inner_cut_width = frame_outer_width - 2 * frame_wall

raised_frame = (
    raised_frame
    .cut(
        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .workplane(offset=plate_thickness)
        .box(inner_cut_length, inner_cut_width, frame_height, centered=(True, True, False))
    )
)

# Cut the central opening through the base plate as well
base_plate = (
    base_plate
    .cut(
        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .box(cutout_length, cutout_width, plate_thickness, centered=(True, True, False))
    )
)

# Combine base plate and raised frame
result = base_plate.union(raised_frame)

# Add cylindrical bosses at the four corners
boss_positions = [
    (boss_x_offset, boss_y_offset),
    (-boss_x_offset, boss_y_offset),
    (boss_x_offset, -boss_y_offset),
    (-boss_x_offset, -boss_y_offset),
]

for (x, y) in boss_positions:
    boss = (
        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .workplane(offset=plate_thickness)
        .center(x, y)
        .circle(boss_radius)
        .extrude(boss_height)
    )
    result = result.union(boss)

# Drill holes in the bosses
for (x, y) in boss_positions:
    hole = (
        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .workplane(offset=plate_thickness)
        .center(x, y)
        .circle(hole_radius)
        .extrude(boss_height)
    )
    result = result.cut(hole)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
total_length = 80  # total length of the part
total_height = 50  # total height at the tallest point
depth = 20  # depth/thickness of the part

# Base rectangular height
base_height = 25
base_length = total_length

# Upper section parameters
slot_width = 15  # width of the U-shaped slot
left_wall_width = 15  # width of left raised wall
right_wall_width = 25  # width of right raised wall
upper_height = total_height  # total height including upper walls

# Angled cut on the left side
angle_cut_height = 25  # height where the angle starts from bottom-left
angle_cut_length = 25  # horizontal distance of the angled cut

# Create the 2D profile
# Starting from bottom-left, going clockwise
# The left side has an angled cut going from bottom-left up to where the left wall starts

profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(0, 0)  # bottom-left (after angle cut)
    .lineTo(total_length, 0)  # bottom-right
    .lineTo(total_length, total_height)  # top-right
    .lineTo(total_length - right_wall_width, total_height)  # top of right wall, inner edge
    .lineTo(total_length - right_wall_width, base_height)  # down to slot bottom
    .lineTo(total_length - right_wall_width - slot_width, base_height)  # across slot bottom
    .lineTo(total_length - right_wall_width - slot_width, total_height - 5)  # up left wall (slightly shorter)
    .lineTo(total_length - right_wall_width - slot_width - left_wall_width, total_height - 5)  # top of left wall
    # Now angle down to the bottom-left
    .lineTo(0, 0)  # angled line back to start
    .close()
    .extrude(depth)
)

# Revised approach - cleaner profile based on image analysis
# The shape has: flat bottom, right side goes straight up to full height,
# a U-slot cut from top, left side has angled/tapered profile

result = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)  # bottom-left corner (lowest point of angle)
    .lineTo(total_length, 0)  # bottom-right
    .lineTo(total_length, total_height)  # top-right corner
    .lineTo(total_length - right_wall_width, total_height)  # across top of right wall
    .lineTo(total_length - right_wall_width, base_height)  # down into slot
    .lineTo(total_length - right_wall_width - slot_width, base_height)  # across slot floor
    .lineTo(total_length - right_wall_width - slot_width, total_height - 8)  # up left wall
    .lineTo(angle_cut_length, total_height - 8)  # top of left wall
    .lineTo(0, 0)  # angled line back to bottom-left
    .close()
    .extrude(depth)
)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
total_length = 100.0
base_width = 30.0
base_height = 20.0
eye_outer_radius = 15.0
eye_inner_radius = 7.0
eye_thickness = base_width
taper_length = 65.0
tip_height = 3.0
tip_width = 8.0

# Create the main body - a tapered wedge shape
# Start with a 2D profile on the XZ plane (side view)
# The part has a rectangular back section that tapers to a point

# Back section height
back_height = base_height
back_length = total_length - taper_length

# Create the side profile as a polygon
# Origin at the back-bottom of the part
profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(back_length, 0)
    .lineTo(back_length, back_height)
    .lineTo(0, back_height)
    .close()
)

# Extrude the back block
back_block = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(back_length, 0)
    .lineTo(back_length, back_height)
    .lineTo(0, back_height)
    .close()
    .extrude(base_width)
)

# Create the tapered wedge section
# This goes from the back block forward, tapering down
wedge_profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(back_length, 0)
    .lineTo(total_length, 0)
    .lineTo(total_length, tip_height)
    .lineTo(back_length, back_height)
    .close()
    .extrude(base_width)
)

# Also taper the width - the tip is narrower
# Create a cutting body to taper the width
# At x=back_length, full width; at x=total_length, narrow width
half_width_diff = (base_width - tip_width) / 2.0

# Right side taper cut
right_taper = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(back_length, base_width)
    .lineTo(total_length, base_width / 2.0 + tip_width / 2.0)
    .lineTo(total_length, base_width + 10)
    .lineTo(back_length, base_width + 10)
    .close()
    .extrude(back_height + 20)
)

# Left side taper cut
left_taper = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(back_length, 0)
    .lineTo(total_length, base_width / 2.0 - tip_width / 2.0)
    .lineTo(total_length, -10)
    .lineTo(back_length, -10)
    .close()
    .extrude(back_height + 20)
)

# Combine the back block and wedge
body = back_block.union(wedge_profile)

# Cut the width tapers
body = body.cut(right_taper).cut(left_taper)

# Create the eye/lug at the back
# The eye is a cylinder with a hole, sitting on top of the back section
# Center of the eye is at x=0, z=back_height, y=base_width/2
eye_center_x = 0.0
eye_center_z = back_height

# Create the cylindrical eye boss
eye_cylinder = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .transformed(offset=cq.Vector(eye_center_x, -base_width/2, eye_center_z))
    .circle(eye_outer_radius)
    .extrude(base_width)
)

# Create the hole through the eye
eye_hole = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .transformed(offset=cq.Vector(eye_center_x, -base_width/2, eye_center_z))
    .circle(eye_inner_radius)
    .extrude(base_width + 10)
)

# Union the eye with the body
body = body.union(eye_cylinder)

# Cut the hole
body = body.cut(eye_hole)

# Cut away material below the base plane (z=0)
cut_below = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .rect(total_length * 3, base_width * 3)
    .extrude(-eye_outer_radius * 2)
)
body = body.cut(cut_below)

result = body
```

```python
import cadquery as cq
```

```python
# Parameters
base_width = 60
base_depth = 60
base_height = 40
upper_width = 60
upper_depth = 35
upper_height = 30
fillet_radius = 20

# Create the base block (L-shaped profile extruded)
# Start with a full base block
base = cq.Workplane("XY").box(base_width, base_depth, base_height, centered=(True, True, False))

# Create the upper block, positioned on top of the base, offset to the back
upper = (cq.Workplane("XY")
         .transformed(offset=(0, -(base_depth - upper_depth) / 2, base_height))
         .box(upper_width, upper_depth, upper_height, centered=(True, True, False)))

# Combine base and upper
result = base.union(upper)

# Now we need to create the concave cylindrical cut at the front-bottom
# The quarter-circle cutout is at the front face, bottom area
# It appears to be a quarter-cylinder cut from the front-right area

# The fillet/concave cut appears at the step transition on the right/front side
# Looking at the image: there's a concave quarter-cylinder where the step is
# on the front-right corner at the height transition

# Create a cylinder to cut the concave quarter-round at the step
# The quarter circle is at the front of the part where the step down occurs
cut_cylinder = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
                .transformed(offset=(0, base_depth/2 - upper_depth, base_height))
                .cylinder(base_width + 10, fillet_radius, centered=(True, True, True)))

# Position the cutting cylinder at the correct location
# The concave cut is at the inner corner of the step
cut_tool = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
            .center(0, base_height)
            .transformed(offset=(0, 0, -(base_depth/2 - upper_depth)))
            .circle(fillet_radius)
            .extrude(base_width + 10, both=True))

# Actually, let me reconsider the geometry from the image
# It's an L-shaped block with a concave quarter-round fillet at the inner step corner

# Create the shape using a 2D profile approach
# Profile in YZ plane (side view): L-shape with quarter-round at inner corner
result = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
          .moveTo(-base_depth/2, 0)
          .lineTo(-base_depth/2, base_height + upper_height)
          .lineTo(-base_depth/2 + upper_depth, base_height + upper_height)
          .lineTo(-base_depth/2 + upper_depth, base_height)
          .radiusArc((base_depth/2 - (base_depth - upper_depth - fillet_radius), base_height - fillet_radius), -fillet_radius)
          .lineTo(base_depth/2, base_height - fillet_radius)
          .lineTo(base_depth/2, 0)
          .close()
          .extrude(base_width/2, both=True))

# Redo with cleaner approach
# Side profile (YZ plane): 
# Bottom-left, top-left, step top-right, quarter circle down, bottom-right
step_x = -base_depth/2 + upper_depth  # x position of the step

result = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
          .moveTo(-base_depth/2, 0)
          .lineTo(-base_depth/2, base_height + upper_height)
          .lineTo(step_x, base_height + upper_height)
          .lineTo(step_x, base_height)
          .threePointArc(
              (step_x + fillet_radius * (1 - 0.7071), base_height - fillet_radius * 0.7071),
              (step_x + fillet_radius, base_height - fillet_radius)
          )
          .lineTo(base_depth/2, base_height - fillet_radius)
          .lineTo(base_depth/2, 0)
          .close()
          .extrude(base_width/2, both=True))
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Chair parameters
seat_width = 400  # mm
seat_depth = 400  # mm
seat_thickness = 20  # mm
seat_height = 450  # mm

leg_size = 35  # mm (square cross-section)
leg_height = seat_height  # mm

backrest_height = 350  # mm (above seat)
backrest_thickness = 20  # mm
back_post_width = 35  # mm

# Create the four legs
# Front-left leg
leg_fl = (cq.Workplane("XY")
          .box(leg_size, leg_size, leg_height)
          .translate((leg_size/2, leg_size/2, leg_height/2)))

# Front-right leg
leg_fr = (cq.Workplane("XY")
          .box(leg_size, leg_size, leg_height)
          .translate((seat_width - leg_size/2, leg_size/2, leg_height/2)))

# Back-left leg (extends up for backrest)
leg_bl = (cq.Workplane("XY")
          .box(leg_size, leg_size, leg_height + backrest_height)
          .translate((leg_size/2, seat_depth - leg_size/2, (leg_height + backrest_height)/2)))

# Back-right leg (extends up for backrest)
leg_br = (cq.Workplane("XY")
          .box(leg_size, leg_size, leg_height + backrest_height)
          .translate((seat_width - leg_size/2, seat_depth - leg_size/2, (leg_height + backrest_height)/2)))

# Create the seat
seat = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .box(seat_width, seat_depth, seat_thickness)
        .translate((seat_width/2, seat_depth/2, seat_height + seat_thickness/2)))

# Backrest top rail
top_rail_z = seat_height + backrest_height - back_post_width/2
top_rail = (cq.Workplane("XY")
            .box(seat_width, backrest_thickness, back_post_width)
            .translate((seat_width/2, seat_depth - leg_size/2, top_rail_z)))

# Backrest middle rail (the horizontal bar with slot)
mid_rail_height = 25  # mm
mid_rail_z = seat_height + backrest_height * 0.65
mid_rail = (cq.Workplane("XY")
            .box(seat_width - 2 * leg_size, backrest_thickness, mid_rail_height)
            .translate((seat_width/2, seat_depth - leg_size/2, mid_rail_z)))

# Create a slot/cutout in the middle rail for the handle detail
slot_width = seat_width - 2 * leg_size - 60  # mm
slot_height = 10  # mm
slot = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .box(slot_width, backrest_thickness + 10, slot_height)
        .translate((seat_width/2, seat_depth - leg_size/2, mid_rail_z)))

mid_rail = mid_rail.cut(slot)

# Combine all parts
result = (leg_fl
          .union(leg_fr)
          .union(leg_bl)
          .union(leg_br)
          .union(seat)
          .union(top_rail)
          .union(mid_rail))

# Add small chamfers/fillets to edges for realism
result = result.edges().chamfer(1.0)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
base_length = 80  # total length in X
base_width = 60   # total width in Y
base_height = 20  # height of the base/lower portion

step_height = 20  # height of the upper stepped portion
step_length = 40  # length of the upper step in X
step_width = 20   # width of the upper step in Y

chamfer_size = 5  # size of the diagonal chamfer/bevel on the inner corner

# Create the L-shaped base profile
# The L-shape when viewed from top: full base, with upper step on one side
base = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(base_length, 0)
    .lineTo(base_length, base_width)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length, base_width)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length, base_width - step_width)
    .lineTo(0, base_width - step_width)
    .close()
    .extrude(base_height)
)

# Create the upper stepped portion (the taller part)
upper_step = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=base_height)
    .moveTo(base_length - step_length, base_width - step_width)
    .lineTo(base_length, base_width - step_width)
    .lineTo(base_length, base_width)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length, base_width)
    .close()
    .extrude(step_height)
)

# Combine base and upper step
result = base + upper_step

# Add the chamfer/ramp feature along the inner L-corner
# The ramp connects the top of the base to the top of the upper step
# along the diagonal inner edge
ramp = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .workplane(offset=base_width - step_width)
    .moveTo(base_length - step_length, base_height)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length, base_height + step_height)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length + step_width, base_height)
    .close()
    .extrude(-step_width)  # extrude in negative Y direction (towards front)
)

# Also create a ramp along the other direction of the L
ramp2 = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .workplane(offset=base_length - step_length)
    .moveTo(base_width - step_width, base_height)
    .lineTo(base_width - step_width, base_height + step_height)
    .lineTo(base_width - step_width - step_width, base_height)
    .close()
    .extrude(-step_width)
)

# Create a diagonal ramp/chamfer that runs along the inner corner
# This appears to be a sloped surface connecting the lower base top to the upper step
ramp_width = 8
ramp_body = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=base_height)
    .moveTo(0, base_width - step_width)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length, base_width - step_width)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length, base_width)
    .lineTo(0, base_width - step_width + ramp_width)
    .close()
    .extrude(step_height)
)

# Simpler approach - create the chamfered ramp as a wedge along the inner edge
# Reset and rebuild with cleaner geometry

result2 = base + upper_step

# Create the diagonal ramp/bevel that connects along the inner L-step
# It looks like a triangular prism running diagonally
bevel = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=base_height)
    .transformed(offset=(0, 0, 0))
    .moveTo(0, base_width - step_width)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length, base_width - step_width)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length, base_width)
    .close()
    .workplane(offset=step_height)
    .moveTo(base_length - step_length, base_width - step_width)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length, base_width)
    .lineTo(base_length - step_length - 0.01, base_width - step_width)
    .close()
    .loft()
)

result = result2 + bevel
```

```python
import cadquery as cq
```

```python
# Parameters
total_length = 120.0
total_height = 50.0
thickness = 10.0

# Left section (tall part) dimensions
left_section_length = 80.0
left_section_height = total_height

# Right section (shorter with legs) dimensions
right_section_length = 40.0
right_top_height = total_height
leg_height = 25.0
leg_width = 10.0
notch_width = 20.0
notch_depth = 25.0

# Top notch dimensions
top_notch_width = 10.0
top_notch_depth = 12.0
top_notch_radius = 5.0

# Create the profile as a 2D sketch on XZ plane
# Build the profile using a wire/sketch approach
# The shape: tall rectangle on left, right side has a U-shaped cutout at bottom
# and a semicircular notch at top

# Create the main profile
profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(total_length, 0)
    .lineTo(total_length, leg_height)
    .lineTo(left_section_length + notch_width + leg_width, leg_height)
    # Right side of notch going up with fillet
    .radiusArc((left_section_length + notch_width, leg_height + 5.0), -5.0)
    .lineTo(left_section_length + notch_width, total_height - top_notch_depth)
    # Top notch (semicircular)
    .radiusArc((left_section_length + notch_width + top_notch_width, total_height - top_notch_depth), -top_notch_radius)
    .lineTo(total_length, total_height - top_notch_depth)
    .lineTo(total_length, total_height)
    .lineTo(0, total_height)
    .lineTo(0, 0)
    .close()
)

# Cut out the U-shaped gap between the legs on the right side
# The gap is between x = left_section_length and x = left_section_length + notch_width
# from y = 0 to y = leg_height

result = (
    profile.extrude(thickness)
)

# Now cut the rectangular notch (the gap between the two legs)
notch_cut = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(left_section_length, 0)
    .rect(notch_width, leg_height * 2, centered=False)
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Rebuild with proper profile
# Let me redo this more carefully

# Profile points (looking at XZ plane, X is length, Z is height)
# Start at bottom-left corner
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    # Bottom edge to right
    .lineTo(left_section_length, 0)
    # Up the left inner leg
    .lineTo(left_section_length, leg_height)
    # Fillet at top of left inner leg
    .radiusArc((left_section_length + 5, leg_height + 5), -5.0)
    # Across the top of the notch
    .lineTo(left_section_length + notch_width - 5, leg_height + 5)
    # Fillet at top of right inner leg  
    .radiusArc((left_section_length + notch_width, leg_height), -5.0)
    # Down the right inner leg
    .lineTo(left_section_length + notch_width, 0)
    # Continue bottom edge to far right
    .lineTo(total_length, 0)
    # Up the right edge
    .lineTo(total_length, total_height)
    # Top edge going left to top notch
    .lineTo(left_section_length + notch_width + top_notch_width / 2 + top_notch_radius, total_height)
    # Top semicircular notch
    .radiusArc((left_section_length + notch_width + top_notch_width / 2 - top_notch_radius, total_height), top_notch_radius)
    # Continue top edge to left
    .lineTo(0, total_height)
    # Down the left edge
    .lineTo(0, 0)
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters for the rectangular frame
outer_width = 120.0    # outer width (X direction)
outer_height = 80.0    # outer height (Y direction)
thickness = 10.0       # thickness/depth (Z direction)
wall_width = 15.0      # width of the frame walls

# Inner cutout dimensions
inner_width = outer_width - 2 * wall_width
inner_height = outer_height - 2 * wall_width

# Corner radius for the inner cutout
inner_fillet_radius = 8.0

# Create the outer rectangular block
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .rect(outer_width, outer_height)
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Cut out the inner rectangular hole with rounded corners
result = (
    result
    .faces(">Z")
    .workplane()
    .rect(inner_width, inner_height)
    .cutThruAll()
)

# Add fillets to the inner cutout edges
result = (
    result
    .edges("|Z")
    .edges(cq.selectors.BoxSelector(
        (-inner_width/2 - 1, -inner_height/2 - 1, -1),
        (inner_width/2 + 1, inner_height/2 + 1, thickness + 1)
    ))
    .fillet(inner_fillet_radius)
)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
# Main cylinder
main_diameter = 50.0
main_height = 80.0

# Top cap/lid - slightly larger diameter, thin disc
cap_diameter = 54.0
cap_height = 5.0

# Boss/hub on top of cap
hub_diameter = 18.0
hub_height = 5.0

# Thin rod/pin extending from hub
rod_diameter = 5.0
rod_height = 30.0

# Build the main cylinder body
main_body = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .circle(main_diameter / 2)
    .extrude(main_height)
)

# Add the top cap
cap = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=main_height)
    .circle(cap_diameter / 2)
    .extrude(cap_height)
)

# Add the hub/boss on top of the cap
hub = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=main_height + cap_height)
    .circle(hub_diameter / 2)
    .extrude(hub_height)
)

# Add the rod/pin extending from the hub
rod = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=main_height + cap_height + hub_height)
    .circle(rod_diameter / 2)
    .extrude(rod_height)
)

# Combine all parts
result = main_body.union(cap).union(hub).union(rod)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
total_length = 200.0
width = 15.0
thickness = 8.0

# Narrowed center section parameters
narrow_width = 10.0
narrow_length = 30.0
taper_length = 10.0

# Notch parameters (two notches visible on the bar)
notch_depth = 2.0
notch_width = 3.0

# Create the base bar profile using a 2D sketch extruded
# The bar has a narrow waist in the center

# Define the profile points for the top view (XY plane)
# Going along the length (X) with varying width (Y)
half_length = total_length / 2
half_width = width / 2
half_narrow = narrow_width / 2
half_narrow_length = narrow_length / 2

# Create the profile as a wire
profile = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-half_length, half_width)
    .lineTo(-half_narrow_length - taper_length, half_width)
    .lineTo(-half_narrow_length, half_narrow)
    .lineTo(half_narrow_length, half_narrow)
    .lineTo(half_narrow_length + taper_length, half_width)
    .lineTo(half_length, half_width)
    .lineTo(half_length, -half_width)
    .lineTo(half_narrow_length + taper_length, -half_width)
    .lineTo(half_narrow_length, -half_narrow)
    .lineTo(-half_narrow_length, -half_narrow)
    .lineTo(-half_narrow_length - taper_length, -half_width)
    .lineTo(-half_length, -half_width)
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Add notches on the top surface
# First notch - on the left side of the bar
notch_pos_1 = -half_length * 0.45
notch1 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=thickness - notch_depth)
    .moveTo(notch_pos_1 - notch_width/2, -half_width - 1)
    .rect(notch_width, width + 2)
    .extrude(notch_depth + 1)
)

# Second notch - on the right side of the bar
notch_pos_2 = half_length * 0.45
notch2 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=thickness - notch_depth)
    .moveTo(notch_pos_2 - notch_width/2, -half_width - 1)
    .rect(notch_width, width + 2)
    .extrude(notch_depth + 1)
)

# Cut the notches from the main body
result = profile.cut(notch1).cut(notch2)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Tensile test specimen (dog bone shape) - ASTM D638 style
# Parameters
total_length = 165.0
grip_width = 19.0
narrow_width = 6.0
thickness = 4.0
grip_length = 40.0
narrow_length = 33.0
transition_length = (total_length - 2 * grip_length - narrow_length) / 2

# Create the dog bone profile using a 2D sketch
# Build the profile as a series of points along the top and bottom edges
# Starting from the left end, going clockwise

# Use workplane and create the profile
half_total = total_length / 2
half_grip = grip_width / 2
half_narrow = narrow_width / 2

# Create the dog bone shape by sketching the profile
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-half_total, -half_grip)
    # Bottom edge - left grip
    .lineTo(-half_total + grip_length, -half_grip)
    # Bottom transition - left
    .tangentArcPoint(
        (-half_total + grip_length + transition_length, -half_narrow),
        relative=False
    )
    # Bottom edge - narrow section
    .lineTo(half_total - grip_length - transition_length, -half_narrow)
    # Bottom transition - right
    .tangentArcPoint(
        (half_total - grip_length, -half_grip),
        relative=False
    )
    # Bottom edge - right grip
    .lineTo(half_total, -half_grip)
    # Right end
    .lineTo(half_total, half_grip)
    # Top edge - right grip
    .lineTo(half_total - grip_length, half_grip)
    # Top transition - right
    .tangentArcPoint(
        (half_total - grip_length - transition_length, half_narrow),
        relative=False
    )
    # Top edge - narrow section
    .lineTo(-half_total + grip_length + transition_length, half_narrow)
    # Top transition - left
    .tangentArcPoint(
        (-half_total + grip_length, half_grip),
        relative=False
    )
    # Top edge - left grip
    .lineTo(-half_total, half_grip)
    # Close
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
total_length = 150.0
end_width = 15.0
end_height = 12.0
narrow_width = 8.0
narrow_height = 8.0
end_section_length = 25.0
taper_length = 25.0

# Create the bar using loft between sections
# The bar has rectangular cross-sections that taper from larger ends to a narrower middle

# Build using workplanes and loft approach
# We'll create this by defining cross-sections along the length (X-axis)

# Define section positions along X
x0 = 0.0
x1 = end_section_length  # end of first rectangular section
x2 = end_section_length + taper_length  # end of first taper
x3 = total_length - end_section_length - taper_length  # start of second taper
x4 = total_length - end_section_length  # start of second rectangular end
x5 = total_length

# First end block
end_block_1 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .center(end_section_length / 2, 0)
    .rect(end_section_length, end_width)
    .extrude(end_height)
    .translate((0, 0, -end_height / 2))
)

# Second end block
end_block_2 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .center(x4 + end_section_length / 2, 0)
    .rect(end_section_length, end_width)
    .extrude(end_height)
    .translate((0, 0, -end_height / 2))
)

# Create tapered section 1 (from end_block_1 to narrow middle) using loft
taper_1 = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .workplane(offset=x1)
    .rect(end_width, end_height)
    .workplane(offset=taper_length)
    .rect(narrow_width, narrow_height)
    .loft()
)

# Create middle narrow section
middle_length = x3 - x2
middle_section = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .center(x2 + middle_length / 2, 0)
    .rect(middle_length, narrow_width)
    .extrude(narrow_height)
    .translate((0, 0, -narrow_height / 2))
)

# Create tapered section 2 (from narrow middle to end_block_2) using loft
taper_2 = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .workplane(offset=x3)
    .rect(narrow_width, narrow_height)
    .workplane(offset=taper_length)
    .rect(end_width, end_height)
    .loft()
)

# Union all parts together
result = end_block_1.union(taper_1).union(middle_section).union(taper_2).union(end_block_2)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
total_length = 150.0
end_width = 15.0
end_height = 15.0
end_section_length = 30.0
middle_width = 10.0
middle_height = 10.0
taper_length = 10.0

# Create the bar using loft sections or by building the profile
# Approach: create the shape by extruding a profile and cutting tapers

# Start with the full rectangular bar
result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(total_length, end_width, end_height)

# Create the necked-down middle section by cutting material
# The middle section is narrower and shorter in cross-section
# We need to remove material from both sides (width) and top/bottom (height)

# Middle section spans from end_section_length to (total_length - end_section_length)
# with tapers at each transition

middle_length = total_length - 2 * end_section_length

# Cut the width reduction on one side (front, +Y side)
cut_depth_y = (end_width - middle_width) / 2.0
cut_depth_z = (end_height - middle_height) / 2.0

# Create a cutting body for the narrowing - using a sketch approach
# We'll cut from +Y side
def make_side_cut():
    """Create a profile in XZ plane to sweep/extrude for side cuts"""
    # Profile along the length (X direction) showing the cut depth (Y direction)
    pts = [
        (-total_length/2, 0),
        (-total_length/2 + end_section_length, 0),
        (-total_length/2 + end_section_length + taper_length, cut_depth_y),
        (total_length/2 - end_section_length - taper_length, cut_depth_y),
        (total_length/2 - end_section_length, 0),
        (total_length/2, 0),
    ]
    return pts

# Cut from +Y side (width reduction)
cut_y_pos = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(0, end_width/2, 0))
    .moveTo(-total_length/2, -end_height/2)
    .lineTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length, -end_height/2)
    .lineTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length + taper_length, -end_height/2)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length - taper_length, -end_height/2)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length, -end_height/2)
    .lineTo(total_length/2, -end_height/2)
    .lineTo(total_length/2, end_height/2)
    .lineTo(-total_length/2, end_height/2)
    .close()
    .extrude(-cut_depth_y)
)

# Actually, let me use a cleaner approach with workplane profiles

# Build the shape as a series of sections
# Use the XZ plane, draw the side profile, extrude in Y

# Side profile (XZ plane) - top cut
top_cut = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length, end_height/2)
    .lineTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length + taper_length, end_height/2 - cut_depth_z)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length - taper_length, end_height/2 - cut_depth_z)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length, end_height/2)
    .lineTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length, end_height/2)
    .close()
    .extrude(end_width)
    .translate((0, -end_width/2, 0))
)

bottom_cut = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length, -end_height/2)
    .lineTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length + taper_length, -end_height/2 + cut_depth_z)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length - taper_length, -end_height/2 + cut_depth_z)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length, -end_height/2)
    .lineTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length, -end_height/2)
    .close()
    .extrude(end_width)
    .translate((0, -end_width/2, 0))
)

# Side cuts (XY plane profile)
front_cut = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length, end_width/2)
    .lineTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length + taper_length, end_width/2 - cut_depth_y)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length - taper_length, end_width/2 - cut_depth_y)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length, end_width/2)
    .close()
    .extrude(end_height)
    .translate((0, 0, -end_height/2))
)

back_cut = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length, -end_width/2)
    .lineTo(-total_length/2 + end_section_length + taper_length, -end_width/2 + cut_depth_y)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length - taper_length, -end_width/2 + cut_depth_y)
    .lineTo(total_length/2 - end_section_length, -end_width/2)
    .close()
    .extrude(end_height)
    .translate((0, 0, -end_height/2))
)

result = result.cut(top_cut).cut(bottom_cut).cut(front_cut).cut(back_cut)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# This appears to be a half-cylinder (semicircular cross-section extruded)
# It's a cylinder cut in half along its length

# Parameters
radius = 40
length = 120

# Create a half-cylinder by making a full cylinder and cutting it in half
# Or better, create a semicircular profile and extrude it

result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .radiusArc((radius * 2, 0), radius)  # semicircle
    .close()  # close with a straight line
    .extrude(length)
)

# Alternative approach - create cylinder and cut in half
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .cylinder(length, radius, centered=(True, True, False))
    .workplane(offset=-1)
    .transformed(offset=(0, 0, -1))
    .workplane()
)

# Cleaner approach: sketch a semicircle and extrude
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .radiusArc((0, radius * 2), radius)
    .close()
    .extrude(length)
)

# Simplest correct approach: half cylinder
radius = 40
height = 120

result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .cylinder(height, radius, centered=(True, True, True))
    .workplane()
    .transformed(offset=(0, 0, 0))
    .workplane(offset=-height/2)
    .center(-radius, 0)
    .rect(radius * 2, radius * 2, centered=False)
    .cutThruAll()
)

# Most straightforward approach
radius = 40
length = 120

# Create semicircular cross-section and extrude
result = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .moveTo(0, -radius)
    .threePointArc((radius, 0), (0, radius))
    .close()
    .extrude(length)
)
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Long thin rectangular bar/rod
# Parameters
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# This appears to be a rectangular block with chamfered/beveled edges on one end
# creating a pentagonal cross-section shape - like a house/barn shape or 
# a rectangular block with angled cuts on the far end

# Parameters
length = 80  # length along the long axis
width = 40   # width of the base
height = 40  # height of the base rectangular portion
chamfer_depth = 20  # how far the angled cut goes

# Create the base rectangular block
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(length, width, height)
)

# The shape appears to be a rectangular block with the top two edges on one end
# chamfered at an angle, creating a beveled/angled face on one end
# It looks like a large chamfer on the far end's vertical edges (right side)

# Actually, looking more carefully, it appears to be a pentagonal prism -
# a rectangular block with a chamfer cutting the top-right vertical edge
# Or more precisely, it's a block with one end having angled/chamfered corners

# Let me create this as an extruded pentagonal profile
# The cross-section from the end looks like a pentagon - 
# rectangle with top corner cut at an angle

# Re-examining: it's a rectangular block with large chamfers on the 
# bottom edges at one end, creating a tapered/beveled end

# It looks like a rectangular prism with the far end having 
# angled faces - like chamfered vertical edges on one end

result = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
```

```python
import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
hex_size = 30.0  # across flats of hex head
hex_height = 35.0
cylinder_od = 25.0  # outer diameter of cylindrical bore section
cylinder_id = 14.0  # inner diameter (bore)
cylinder_length = 45.0
wall_thickness = (cylinder_od - cylinder_id) / 2

# Create the hexagonal head block
# Hex prism - using polygon with 6 sides
hex_head = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .polygon(6, hex_size * 2 / 3**0.5)  # circumscribed radius from across-flats
    .extrude(hex_height)
)

# Move hex head so its bottom is at the cylinder center height
# The cylinder will go through the hex block horizontally
# Position: cylinder axis along X, hex head rises in Z

# Let's rebuild with proper orientation
# Cylinder along Y axis, hex head on top

# Create the main cylindrical body along Y axis
cylinder_body = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .circle(cylinder_od / 2)
    .extrude(cylinder_length)
)

# Create the bore through the cylinder
bore = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .circle(cylinder_id / 2)
    .extrude(cylinder_length + 10)  # extra to ensure clean cut
    .translate((0, -5, 0))
)

# Create hex head - positioned at the back end of the cylinder, rising above
# The hex head sits on top/around the back portion of the cylinder
hex_af = 32.0  # across flats
hex_h = 30.0

hex_head = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .polygon(6, hex_af / (3**0.5) * 2 / 2 * 1.1547)
    .extrude(hex_h)
    .translate((0, cylinder_length - hex_h * 0.6, -hex_af / 4))
)

# Let me redo this more carefully
# The part appears to be an elbow fitting or cable connector:
# - A horizontal cylinder (hollow) 
# - A hex nut head at one end, oriented perpendicular (vertical)
# - A rectangular key/flat on top of the cylinder near the hex

# Redo from scratch
result_parts = cq.Workplane("XY")

# Parameters refined
cyl_outer_r = 13.0
cyl_inner_r = 7.0
cyl_length = 50.0
hex_across_flats = 32.0
hex_height = 32.0
key_width = 16.0
key_length = 20.0
key_height = 3.0

# Hex head - vertical, at back of cylinder
hex_body = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")  # vertical plane
    .polygon(6, hex_across_flats / 2 / 0.866)  # circumradius from across-flats
    .extrude(hex_height)
    .translate((0, cyl_length - hex_height, 0))
)

# Cylindrical body along Y axis
cyl_body = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .circle(cyl_outer_r)
    .extrude(cyl_length)
)

# Union hex and cylinder
body = hex_body.union(cyl_body)

# Bore hole through cylinder along Y axis
bore_hole = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .circle(cyl_inner_r)
    .extrude(cyl_length + 20)
    .translate((0, -10, 0))
)

body = body.cut(bore_hole)

# Rectangular key/flat on top of cylinder
key = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=cyl_outer_r)
    .rect(key_width, key_length)
    .extrude(key_height)
    .translate((0, cyl_length / 2 - 5, 0))
)

body = body.union(key)

# Chamfer the top edges of the hex
# Add a small chamfer to the top of hex head
body = body.edges("|Y").edges(
    cq.selectors.BoxSelector(
        (-20, cyl_length - hex_height - 1, 10),
        (20, cyl_length + 1, 25)
    )
).chamfer(3.0)

result = body
```
